model_narrative: We allocate a set of GNMA mortgage pools to fill forward TBA delivery contracts. Each contract is filled through a sequence of delivery lots of fixed target size, and for each pool we decide how much of its face value to place into each contract's lot and how much to leave unallocated in the box. We also decide which lots to fill versus fail, which pools are placed in which lots, and which pools end up split across destinations. The objective is to maximize total profit, which rewards allocated face value by the spread between each contract's forward selling price and the current market price while charging a tiny penalty for splitting pools.

sets.name: p
sets.doc: the available GNMA mortgage pools, each identified by a pool number

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the TBA forward contracts that must be satisfied with delivered pools

sets.name: l
sets.doc: the delivery lots, given in fill order; for each contract the early lots are filled to a fixed size before the next lot receives anything

sets.name: c3
sets.doc: the subset of pools flagged as class-3 high-risk; these pools are restricted in how they may be used to fill a lot

params.name: head
params.doc: the first incremental allocation step size, in face-value units

params.name: steps
params.doc: the incremental step size used when building up an allocation, in face-value units

params.name: pv
params.doc: pool face values keyed by pool and attribute; the attribute 'original' gives the original face value and 'adjusted' gives the adjusted face value, both in face-value units

params.name: tbainfo
params.doc: contract attributes keyed by contract and attribute name: 'quantity' is the total face value the contract must be filled with, 'selling' and 'market' are the forward selling price and current market price quoted per 100 of face value, 'variation' is the fractional tolerance allowed around a lot's target size, and 'maxpools' is the baseline cap on how many distinct pools may be placed in a full-size lot

params.name: a
params.doc: the adjusted face value of each pool, in face-value units; this is the total amount of the pool that is available to be allocated or boxed

params.name: o
params.doc: the original face value of each pool, in face-value units

vars.name: profit
vars.doc: the total profit, in price-spread-weighted face-value units

vars.name: v
vars.doc: the face-value amount of a pool allocated to a given contract's lot

vars.name: b
vars.doc: the adjusted face value of a pool left unallocated in the box

vars.name: z
vars.doc: 1 if any of a pool is allocated to a given contract's lot, 0 otherwise

vars.name: w
vars.doc: 1 if a contract's lot is filled (passes), 0 if the lot is failed and left empty

vars.name: s
vars.doc: 1 if a pool is split across more than one destination, 0 otherwise

description: For each contract lot, when the lot is filled the total face value of all pools placed into that lot must reach at least its target size, allowing the lot to come in slightly under target by the contract's tolerance fraction. If the lot is failed it carries no such lower requirement.

description: For each pool and contract lot, the face value of that pool placed into that lot can be positive only when the pool is marked as allocated to that lot, and even then it cannot exceed the largest amount that lot could hold at the top of its tolerance band.

description: Total profit is the face value allocated to each contract's lots valued at that contract's spread between its forward selling price and the current market price, summed over every pool and lot, reduced by a tiny penalty charged for each pool that is split.
